problem_text: Determine whether integral_2^infinity 1/(x (ln x)^2) dx converges, and if so find its value.

content.label: Improper: infinite upper limit
content.applies_when: the upper limit is infinite, so the integral is defined as a limit of proper integrals.

content.meaning: let u = ln x, du = (1/x) dx; the limits map x=2 -> u=ln 2 and x->infinity -> u->infinity.

content.label: rewrite integral_2^infinity 1/(x (ln x)^2) dx = lim_{s->infinity} integral over the transformed variable
content.action: rewrite integral_2^infinity 1/(x (ln x)^2) dx = lim_{s->infinity} integral over the transformed variable
content.purpose: convert the improper integral into a limit of proper integrals

content.label: substitute u = ln x to transform the integral into integral_{ln 2}^infinity u^-2 du
content.action: substitute u = ln x to transform the integral into integral_{ln 2}^infinity u^-2 du
content.purpose: reduce the integrand to a simple power of u

content.label: the antiderivative of u^-2 is -1/u, so [ -1/u ]_{ln 2}^s = -1/s + 1/ln 2; as s->infinity this -> 1/ln 2
content.action: the antiderivative of u^-2 is -1/u, so [ -1/u ]_{ln 2}^s = -1/s + 1/ln 2; as s->infinity this -> 1/ln 2
content.purpose: evaluate and resolve the limit to the value 1/ln 2
